target.name: Matic Robotics
target.founder: Navneet Dalal, Mehul Nariyawala, Shafaq Presswala

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Matic Robotics is a Series A-stage consumer tech company founded by Navneet Dalal, Mehul Nariyawala, and Shafaq Presswala. The company has raised $107M to date. The comparable universe for this valuation consists of one identified private peer, Daylight Computer, with no public comps available in the consumer tech robotics space.

Stage-progression methodology was applied, using total capital raised ($107M) as a proxy anchor, normalized to a $10M base value representative of typical Series A entry economics. Applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier—standard for progression from Series A to subsequent rounds—yields an implied valuation of $20M. This method is the primary triangulation approach given data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M (low) to $23M (median to high), centered at $20M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method and limited comparable data. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the stage-progression multiplier for consumer robotics specifically, the adequacy of a one-company peer set, and the absence of public market benchmarks. A more robust valuation would require additional private comparable transactions or revenue/unit economics data to stress-test the stage-based approach.
